data.title: Sime Darby: 2Q Net Profit Fell on Weaker Motors Segment Rev, Absence of Asset Disposal Gains
Ratchthani Leasing Appears Set for Asset-Quality Recovery — Market Talk
data.description: Ratchthani Leasing appears set to enter an asset-quality recovery cycle, according to Thanachart Securities' Sarachada Sornsong in a research report, as the brokerage reaffirms its buy rating on the stock. The Thai company has taken a more cautious approach to new lending and strengthened its debt collection processes since 2Q 2023, bringing its balance sheet clean-up to near completion, the analyst notes. The financial services provider is expected to maintain its prudent stance, prioritizing loan quality over aggressive portfolio expansion. However, the brokerage lowers its 2025-2027 earnings estimates for Ratchthani Leasing by average 52%, citing factors such as elevated provisions. The target price is reduced to THB1.84 from THB2.64. Shares are 1.2% higher at THB1.66. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Monday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Warren Buffett Occasionally Writes to Shareholders About Overheated Markets. Not This Year.

The Berkshire Hathaway chairman acknowledged that "often, nothing looks compelling" in the hunt for equity investments.

Australia's Perpetual Ends Talks With KKR Over $1.39B Asset Sale

Australia's Perpetual ended talks with KKR about a potential asset sale to the U.S. buyout firm, and will instead look to hold onto its corporate trust unit.

The Long Nights and Drug Addiction That Drove a Banker to Insider Trading

John Femenia says he conceived of the scheme while high on stimulants and working late as an investment banker.

As banks retreat from DEI, Citi is making these changes to its diversity and hiring practices

Citigroup says it will no longer require "diverse slates of candidates and diverse panels of interviewers."

Coinbase Says SEC Intends to Drop Lawsuit Against Crypto Exchange

The agency had sought to oversee the company as a stock market.

Khosla Ventures Hits Fundraising Trail

Vinod Khosla's firm Khosla Ventures, a leading backer of artificial intelligence and other startups, has started reaching out to potential investors for its next set of funds, according to people familiar with the situation.

Crypto exchange Bybit suffers $1.4 billion hack. Here's why it's troubling for the industry.

The hack of Bybit is the latest incident in which the crypto industry has been targeted.

Why Is Warren Buffett Hoarding So Much Cash?

Investors are poised to study the Berkshire Hathaway chairman's annual letter for insights about the stock market.

The Latest Dilemma Facing Finance Chiefs: What to Tell Investors About Tariffs

CFOs are weighing how much information to share about the impact of tariffs, especially as many are yet to take effect and the landscape continues to evolve.

Private Equity Sees Deal Pipelines Filling In as Year Begins

Private-equity executives entered 2025 with high hopes for a dealmaking recovery that is lifting payouts to fund investors and powering fundraising.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Monday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Oil prices end lower, erasing weekly gains

"A number of decisions are pending ... that could drive the oil price in one direction or the other," one analyst noted.

Trump Paralyzes the U.S. Wind Power Industry

The president, who despises wind turbines, has paused federal permits and leasing for such projects, putting company plans in limbo.

Africa's Richest Man and His $20 Billion Oil Refinery From Hell

After making billions in cement, Nigerian Aliko Dangote is tackling oil and corruption in his quest to become Africa's Rockefeller.

Fossil-Fuel Companies Are Lobbying Trump to Save a Green-Fuel Program

Oil-and-gas companies are pushing the new administration to keep hydrogen tax credits, despite the president's ambition to destroy Biden-era climate incentives.

Trump's Energy Plans Are Creating Surprising Opportunities

It's not all about oil, one of the president's top priorities. Why natural gas leads our list of the industry's most promising investments. Even some solar and wind stocks could climb.

Adnoc Sells $2.84 Billion of Shares in Gas Subsidiary

The move is to increase Adnoc Gas's free float and paves the way for the subsidiary's inclusion in share indexes.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories increased by more than expected last week in a fourth consecutive build, while product stocks declined, according to U.S. government data.

How Tariffs Could Shock America's Power System

Transformers used in power grids are especially vulnerable to trade disruptions.

Schneider Electric Beats Market Views

The energy-management and automation group posted higher net profit and revenue for 2024 on continued strength in energy management, beating market views.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Read about the U.S. rig count, Sembcorp Industries, Petronas Dagangan, and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0242 GMT - Iron ore falls in early Asian trade. The ferrous metal's fundamentals appear to have strong support, say Nanhua Futures analysts in a note. On the valuation side, market sentiment has recovered after recent data showed strong steel consumption in China, they write. Investors are also awaiting China's National People's Congress meeting in March for any additional property-supportive measures. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.4% lower at 794.5 yuan a ton.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0239 GMT - Palm oil prices fall in Asian trading, tracking weakness in soybean oil last Friday on the Chicago Board of Trade, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Palm olein's softness on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is also adding pressure to palm oil, it reckons. Major consumer India may raise vegetable oil import taxes to support struggling local oilseed farmers, which could boost local prices but curb imports of palm oil, soybean oil and sunflower oil, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support for CPO prices at 4,610 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,705 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 51 ringgit to 4,613 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0132 GMT - Copper is lower in early Asian trade. Bullish factors may still dominate in the short term with prices supported by expectations of tight supply, say Nanhua Futures analysts in a commentary. However, in the medium term, potential downside risks may emerge on weaker demand due to tariff policies and gradual easing of supply constraints, the analysts note. The three-month LME copper contract is 0.5% lower at $9,508.00 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

2355 GMT - Gold edges higher in the early Asian session, supported by Friday's weak U.S. economic data that may enhance the haven appeal of the precious metal. The S&P flash U.S. services PMI for February came in at 49.7, slipping into contractionary territory, while the University of Michigan's index of consumer sentiment tumbled to 64.7 at the end of February, well below January's 71.7. Demand for gold is being driven mainly by Western investors and central banks, Commerzbank Research's Carsten Fritsch says in a research report. ETF investors also seem to be jumping on the bandwagon, the commodity analyst adds. Spot gold is 0.1% higher at $2,937.77/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2233 GMT - Gold Road Resources's decision to pay a dividend below market expectations could signal that M&A is on its radar, suggests Barrenjoey. Gold Road declared a final dividend of 1.5 Australian cents/share for a full year payout of 2 cents/share. Analyst Daniel Morgan says the dividend represented 25% of free cash flow in FY 2024 despite Gold Road having A$166 million cash on hand at the end of December and no debt. "This may indicate a desire to grow through inorganic growth," Barrenjoey says. It retains an underweight call on Gold Road, which has risen more than 70% in value over the past 12 months. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Stock Futures Rise on Conservatives Win in German Elections
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

EU CPI; Germany ifo business sentiment; trading update from Sasol

Opening Call:

European stock futures gained; Asian equities are mixed; the dollar weakened; oil futures fell and gold rose

Equities:

European stock futures were tracking higher at the start of the week amid risk-on sentiment as investors reacted to the outcome of Germany's elections with the conservative Christian Democrat Union winning the most votes and looking to form a coalition government.

In the U.S., concerns about a weakening American consumer sent stocks lower last week.

This week's economic highlights include the release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index for January, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, on Friday.

Nvidia's fourth-quarter results on Wednesday are the big earnings to watch this week. BofA analysts said in a recent note that the results will mark the "next important test for AI bulls."

Forex:

The euro gained against other currencies in the early Asian session as traders digested the outcome of Germany's election.

"However, it is unclear whether two or three parties will be needed to form a coalition government," CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research team said.

Market participants will likely be closely watching whether the eventual government agrees to reform the 'debt brake' to support economic growth, the team added.

--

The U.S. dollar weakened amid prospects of Fed rate cuts as U.S. economic data released last Friday were disappointing, NAB said.

The weak U.S. data led to the overnight-index-swap market bringing forward a 25 bp rate cut by the Fed to July from September, Catril added.

Bonds:

With uncertainties mounting around Fed policy and tariffs, fixed income requires caution, said Dan Genter of Genter Capital.

He likes Treasurys with maturities up to seven years. "We are not going to push too much far out into the curve." Long-term bond prices, which fell as yields rose in recent months, might look attractive, but "it's too unclear, with regards to the direction of the tail of the curve," Genter said.

The uncertainty basically stems from a lack of clarity on how tariffs and other Trump policies will affect inflation. Genter expects the inflationary impact to be minimal, but until it becomes more clear to investors "there is no direction at all."

Energy:

Oil prices fell, likely weighed by prospects of more supply. The Trump administration is reportedly pressuring Iraq to resume Kurdish crude oil exports to Turkey, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia said.

Also, recent data from the EIA reinforce oil prices' bearish trend, the research strategist said adding that the increase of 4.6 million barrels in U.S. crude inventories for the week ended Feb. 14 suggests weaker-than-expected domestic demand.

Metals:

Gold edged higher supported by Friday's weak U.S. economic data that may enhance the haven appeal of the precious metal.

Demand for gold is being driven mainly by Western investors and central banks, Commerzbank Research's Carsten Fritsch said. ETF investors also seem to be jumping on the bandwagon, the commodity analyst added.

--

Copper was lower but bullish factors may still dominate in the short term with prices supported by expectations of tight supply, said Nanhua Futures analysts.

However, in the medium term, potential downside risks may emerge on weaker demand due to tariff policies and gradual easing of supply constraints, the analysts added.

--

Iron ore fell in Asian trade. The ferrous metal's fundamentals appear to have strong support, Nanhua Futures analysts said.

On the valuation side, market sentiment has recovered after recent data showed strong steel consumption in China. Investors are also awaiting China's National People's Congress meeting in March for any additional property-supportive measures.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

The U.S. Economy Depends More Than Ever on Rich People

Many Americans are pinching pennies, exhausted by high prices and stubborn inflation. The well-off are spending with abandon.

The top 10% of earners-households making about $250,000 a year or more-are splurging on everything from vacations to designer handbags, buoyed by big gains in stocks, real estate and other assets.

What Do Mass Federal Layoffs Mean for the Labor Market?

America's biggest employer, the federal government, is laying off workers in droves. Depending on how many, and which, jobs get cut, the blow to the U.S. job market could be merely glancing, or seriously damaging.

As of January, the U.S. government had 2.4 million civilian employees, excluding the postal service. That is a lot of people, though just 1.5% of total nonfarm jobs. So what will it mean for the economy if hundreds of thousands of those jobs go away?

The 'next important test for AI bulls' happens this week with Nvidia's earnings

Shares of Nvidia Corp., the chip-making giant at the core of the artificial-intelligence boom, have recovered - somewhat - from the hit they took during the market's DeepSeek tantrum last month. But as the $3.4 trillion company prepares to report fourth-quarter results on Wednesday, Wall Street has other anxieties.

For one, as MarketWatch noted last week, the results will offer a closer look at its new Blackwell chip architecture. Production has ramped up, but the products have faced concerns over shortages, delays and issues with overheating that have reportedly prompted big customers put off orders.

Ukraine's Zelensky Wants Better Terms on Minerals Deal Demanded by Trump

KYIV, Ukraine-The U.S. and Ukraine pushed divergent messages over the weekend, with Kyiv pressing for better terms to the mineral-rights deal sought by President Trump, while Washington urged Ukraine to swiftly accept the proposal already on the table.

The dispute over the minerals proposal is part of mounting disagreements between the U.S. and its allies about how best to end the war in Ukraine. The leaders of France and the U.K. were set to head to Washington this week to lobby on behalf of Ukraine, as the Trump administration's position continues to stretch trans-Atlantic tensions to their greatest in years.

Conservative Friedrich Merz Is Poised to Win German Election

BERLIN-Friedrich Merz is the clear winner of the German election. The question now for the conservative leader is how fast and with whom he can cobble together a government-and whether the U.S. will seek to influence the process.

Despite a historically strong showing by antiestablishment nationalists in a ballot that extended Europe's recent lurch to the right, early estimates indicated that Merz's Christian Democratic Union was on the way to a comfortable victory.

Tesla, Uber Head Toward a Showdown in Austin

The battle over self-driving cars and the future of ride-hailing is slated to start in Austin, Texas, in just a few weeks.

How it goes can help investors figure out how trillions of dollars in market value will be allocated.

How Porsche, Volvo and Rivian Are Injecting Unique Driving Styles Into Self-Driving Cars

A Porsche programmed with aggressive, sporty moves; a Volvo tuned to a more cautious pace; an autonomous Rivian that takes the scenic route, on-road or off.

In the future, cars won't only drive autonomously: Using software and artificial intelligence, their makers will give them distinct driving styles.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Monday

08:00/SVK: Jan Harmonized CPI

08:00/AUT: Jan CPI

09:00/POL: Jan Retail Sales

09:00/POL: Jan Agricultural prices

09:00/GER: Feb ifo Business Climate Index

10:00/MLT: Jan Harmonised CPI

10:00/CYP: Jan Harmonised CPI

10:00/EU: Jan Harmonised CPI

10:00/CRO: Jan CPI

13:00/POL: Jan Broad money M3

14:00/BEL: Feb Business Confidence Survey

15:59/UKR: Jan PPI

17:59/UK: Jan Adzuna UK Job Market Report

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Stabilus CFO Resigns; Interim Successor Named
data.description:
Stabilus Chief Financial Officer Stefan Bauerreis resigned and will leave the company at the end of February.

In light of the resignation, Chief Executive Officer Michael Büchsner will assume the responsibilities of CFO on an interim basis, the German industrial motion control solutions provider noted in a Feb. 21 release.

data.title: Zeotech Executes Nonbinding Letter of Intent for Manufacturing Facilities at Queensland Port
data.description:
Zeotech executed a nonbinding letter of intent with Gladstone Ports to develop AusPozz manufacturing and bulk storage facilities at the port of Bundaberg in Queensland, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The letter has an 18-month term and will expire on August 20, 2026, the filing said.

It provides a framework and development gateways for the parties to consider whether the land and the port facilities are suitable for the potential project, per the filing.

data.title: Sime Darby: 2Q Net Profit Down 87% on Year
Many Peaks Minerals  Says Drilling Results Confirm Mineralization at Ivory Coast Gold Project, Shares Fall 5%
data.description:
Many Peaks Minerals said assays from reconnaissance air core drilling at the Odienné gold project in Ivory Coast confirm gold mineralization on previously undrilled targets, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

It identified multiple mineralized structures for follow-up exploration. The program was completed in December 2024 and comprised 8,089 meters of drilling in 240 drill holes.

It plans to conduct 12,000 meters of follow-up drilling on priority targets.

The firm's shares fell 5% in recent trading on Monday.

data.title: OCI Energy, Israel’s APC to establish JV for solar farm project
data.description: OCI Energy Co., the U.S. subsidiary of South Korea’s OCI Co., announced on Monday that it has signed an agreement with Israel’s Arava Power Co. (APC) to establish a joint venture for a project that involves building a solar photovoltaic farm in Wharton County, Texas, United States. The Sun Roper project involves the construction of a 260-megawatt utility-scale solar power plant on a 693-square-meter site in Wharton County. The plant is capable of generating enough electricity to power approximately 60,000 households in case of Korea for a day. OCI Energy and APC holds an equal stake in the joint venture. The project is expected to be highly profitable, as it is eligible for 30 percent investment tax credits under the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) and an additional 10 percent energy community bonus.

data.title: Hyundai Rotem widens global reach with advanced railway tech
data.description: Hyundai Rotem Co., the railway system manufacturing unit of Hyundai Motor Group, is expanding its global presence with a series of contracts signed to manufacture high-speed rail vehicles and automated light rail vehicles. On Thursday, Maeil Business Newspaper visited Hyundai Rotem’s plant in Changwon, South Gyeongsang Province, where various trains, including those for export and domestic use, are being manufactured. “Hyundai Rotem is a company that possesses both loco-hauled and multiple unit high-speed train technologies,” said Lee Do-kyung, an official from the company’s railway production management team. In July 2024, the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy newly designated the Changwon plant’s high-speed rail vehicle manufacturing technology as a national core technology, leading to enhanced security and surveillance measures at the production facilities. In 2004, Korea became the fourth country in the world to operate high-speed rail in 2004, following Japan (1964), France (1981), and Germany (1991). Initially developed using the French TGV, Korea‘s high-speed railway system has since secured its own technology. In June 2024, Hyundai Rotem signed a contract worth 270 billion won ($188 million) to supply high-speed rail vehicles to Uzbekistan for the first time. The deal includes 42 units of the UTY EMU-250, an upgraded version of the KTX-Eum tailored to travel Uzbekistan’s operating conditions. Uzbekistan’s high-speed rail network covers longer distances and often operates in extreme conditions. “We tailor the trains’ exterior coatings and interior features to suit the local environment,” Lee said. Hyundai Rotem’s railway division reported sales of 1.5 trillion won in 2024. The company secured orders worth 4.78 trillion won. Its order backlog stands at 14.06 trillion won. The Changwon plant is producing automated light rail vehicles for export to Taiwan and electric trains for Singapore under a supply contract. Taiwan is set to receive 80 automated light rail cars by 2029 while the 186 electric train cars ordered by Singapore have been produced sequentially since last year and are being delivered.

data.title: CJ CheilJedang nears $696 mn in annual sales of Hetbahn instant rice
data.description: South Korea’s CJ CheilJedang Corp. is flying high with its Hetbahn instant rice in the United States, with annual sales approaching 1 trillion won ($696 million). According to CJ CheilJedang on Sunday, Hetbahn generated 914.6 billion won in sales in 2024, surpassing 900 billion won for the first time. Overseas markets accounted for 24 percent (223.1 billion won), with 84 percent (188.4 billion won) of exports coming from North America. Hetbahn sales have steadily grown from 688 billion won in 2021 to 815.2 billion won in 2022, 850.3 billion won in 2023, and 914.6 billion won in 2024. Exports more than doubled in three years, from 98.8 billion won in 2021 to 223.1 billion won in 2024. Total cumulative sales of Hetbahn have reached 6 billion units as of the end of last year, with distribution expanding to 40 countries, including the U.S., Australia, Mexico, Canada, and China. In the U.S., Hetbahn?marketed under the Bibigo brand?can be found in major retailers such as Costco and Walmart, as well as in smaller Asian grocery stores and convenience stores in metropolitan areas. CJ CheilJedang uses the Bibigo brand for its instant rice exports, following the success of Bibigo dumplings in the U.S. The company attributes Hetbahn’s growing popularity in the U.S. to the increasing number of Americans consuming rice. As rice gains recognition as a healthier carbohydrate alternative to bread, demand has risen. In a survey conducted by CJ CheilJedang in the U.S. last year, 34.6 percent of respondents cited health benefits as their primary reason for choosing rice. The company also noted that more Americans are pairing instant rice with proteins such as meat, fish, and tofu instead of bread. The global Korean food trend has also fueled Hetbahn’s growth. With more Korean restaurants opening in the U.S., an increasing number of consumers are seeking authentic Korean-style rice at home. CJ CheilJedang has leveraged this trend by highlighting Bibigo rice’s convenience?requiring only two minutes in the microwave?and its ability to be stored at room temperature for extended periods.

data.title: Naver Pay, Kakao Pay, and Toss Pay expand overseas payments
data.description: South Korea’s overseas payments via three major e-wallet payment services have grown exponentially as the demand for international travel surges. Overseas payments by Korean consumers using Naver Pay, Kakao Pay, and Toss Pay soared by 443 percent over the past two years, rising from 117.73 billion won ($81.9 million) in 2022 to 640.31 billion won in 2024. Korean major mobile app payment providers have scrambled to enter global markets as they face increasingly fierce competition from credit card companies in domestic markets. Among the three, Kakao Pay, a digital wallet service run by Kakao Corp., showed the most remarkable growth as its overseas payments skyrocketing from 941 million won in 2022 to 65.59 billion won in 2024. The company has ramped up its global expansion with strategic partnerships with Alipay, which has an extensive network of vendors in China, and PayPay, a Japanese company owned by LY Corp. that develops electronic payment services. Toss Pay recorded the highest volume of payments, which totaled 552.61 billion won in 2024 according to the company. Toss Pay continues to demonstrate strong and consistent growth, not only in Asia but in the United States as well.

data.title: LG’s new video campaign raises awareness on climate change
data.description: LG Electronics is expanding its environmental protection campaign as part of its environmental, social, and governance (ESG) efforts to protect endangered species. The company said on Sunday that it unveiled the fifth video in its campaign, which showcases the monarch butterfly, on a digital billboard in New York’s Times Square. The video highlights the environmental threat facing the butterfly, whose population is rapidly declining due to climate change. Monarch butterflies, which migrate between southern Canada and Mexico, are struggling as their habitats are increasingly affected by global warming.

data.title: CE Capital Partners Abandons Talks to Buy HKM Steel JV, Thyssenkrupp Says
data.description:
Thyssenkrupp said CE Capital Partners terminated discussions regarding the potential acquisition of the HKM steel joint venture, Reuters reported Feb. 21, citing Thyssenkrupp Steel Europe.

The German industrial engineering and steel manufacturing group said a possible sale of the joint venture will remove some uncertainty surrounding the joint venture and its roughly 3,000 employees.

The group noted that it will discuss the situation with its shareholders, adding that it remains open to talks with other potential buyers.

data.title: GCL Energy Technology to Buy Partnership Fund for 3.4 Million Yuan; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
GCL Energy Technology , through subsidiary Suzhou GCL Xinguang Smart Energy, plans to acquire a 99.974% equity stake in Zhike (Zibo) Equity Investment Fund Partnership's (Limited Partnership) for 3.4 million yuan

GCL Xinguang will acquire shares in and participate in the restructuring of the fund, which will result in it acquiring nearly the entire ownership of the fund.

The investment involves the acquisition of existing partnership shares from three entities: Chenfan Wisdom (Hainan) Technology, Yikehui (Qingdao) Private Equity Fund Management, and Shanhai New Energy (Beijing) Energy Technology.

The fund focuses on equity investment and asset management.

Shandong Shunheng Private Equity Fund Management will own 0.026% of the fund following the deal.

GCL Energy's shares rose more than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Coromandel Engineering Gets Board Nod for Proposed Preferential Issue
data.description:
Coromandel Engineering's board approved raising funds through a preferential issue of up to 6,722,722 equity shares at 40.05 Indian rupees per share, which includes a 30.05 rupee premium, according to an Indian bourse filing on Saturday.

The shares will be allotted to individuals under the promoter category and certain non-promoter investors, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

data.description: • BELIMO - 2025 EBIT MARGIN IS EXPECTED TO REMAIN WITHIN THE COMMUNICATED RANGE BETWEEN 18% AND 20%

data.title: Singapore Inflation Eased in January
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

SINGAPORE--Singapore's consumer inflation and the closely watched core measure both cooled in January, showing that price pressures are aligning with policymakers' aims.

The consumer price index rose 1.2% last month from a year ago, the Department of Statistics said Monday. That compared with December's revised 1.5% increase and the median estimate for a rise of 1.95% in a Wall Street Journal poll of six economists.

Core CPI, which strips out private road transport and accommodation costs, rose 0.8% in January, the data showed. That was slower than December's 1.8% growth and the 1.6% expansion expected of five economists in the WSJ poll.

The data came after the Monetary Authority of Singapore in January eased its monetary policy for the first time in five years. A continuous slowing in core inflation may back the case for the central bank to further ease its policy stance, which is centered on the city-state's exchange rate.

While the MAS doesn't have a set target for inflation, it views core inflation of just under 2% on average as consistent with overall price stability in the economy.

 
 
Sector                         Index Weighing         % Change YoY 
Housing & Utilities                    29.38%                 +1.4 
Food                                   20.42%                 +1.5 
Transport                              13.07%                 +3.1 
Recreation, Sport & Culture             5.95%                 -0.7 
Education                               5.79%                 +0.1 
Misc Goods & Services                   4.38%                 +0.2

Singapore Inflation Rate Lowest in Near 2 Years
data.description:
Singapore’s annual inflation rate fell to 1.2% in January 2025 from 1.4% in the previous month, below market consensus of 2.2%.

It was the lowest reading since February 2021.
